## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-31 22:27:19
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## 任务理解

修复 `prompt_toolkit/input/vt100_parser.py` 中 `Vt100Parser._call_handler` 方法的一个 bug：当 `key` 是 tuple 时（例如 `"\x1b[1;3D"` 映射到 `(Keys.Escape, Keys.Left)`），当前代码对 tuple 中的每个 key 都传递相同的 `insert_text`，但正确行为应该是：第一个 key 传递原始 `insert_text`，后续 key 传递空字符串 `""`。

## 问题定位

文件：`code/prompt_toolkit/input/vt100_parser.py`，第 176-178 行：

```python
if isinstance(key, tuple):
    for k in key:
        self._call_handler(k, insert_text)  # 错误：所有 key 都用相同的 insert_text
```

## 修复方案

将循环改为 `enumerate`，对索引 0 的 key 传递原始 `insert_text`，其余传递 `""`：

```python
if isinstance(key, tuple):
    for i, k in enumerate(key):
        self._call_handler(k, insert_text if i == 0 else "")
```

## 预期结果

- `stream.feed("\x1b[1;3D")` 产生 2 个 KeyPress：
  - `KeyPress(Keys.Escape, data="\x1b[1;3D")`
  - `KeyPress(Keys.Left, data="")`

## 测试计划

- 测试命令：`docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests/test_inputstream.py --tb=short -q`
- 目标测试：`test_special_double_keys`（验证新行为）
- 回归测试：`test_control_keys`, `test_arrows`, `test_escape`, `test_flush_1`, `test_flush_2`, `test_meta_arrows`, `test_control_square_close`, `test_invalid`, `test_cpr_response`, `test_cpr_response_2`（共 10 个，全部需通过）

Not in Pygments. elif part == "blink": @@ -338,6 +343,7 @@ bgcolor=_or("", *[a.bgcolor for a in list_of_attrs]), bold=_or(False, *[a.bold for a in list_of_attrs]), underline=_or(False, *[a.underline for a in list_of_attrs]), + strike=_or(False, *[a.strike for a in list_of_attrs]), italic=_or(False, *[a.italic for a in list_of_attrs]), blink=_or(False, *[a.blink for a in list_of_attrs]), reverse=_or(False, *[a.reverse for a in list_of_attrs]), diff -ru --exclude=__pycache__ --exclude=.git --exclude=*.pyc --exclude=.dual-agent --exclude=.pytest_cache --exclude=tests prompt_toolkit/widgets/base.py prompt_toolkit/widgets/base.py --- prompt_toolkit/widgets/base.py 2026-03-31 21:33:23 +++ prompt_toolkit/widgets/base.py 2026-03-31 21:51:18 @@ -361,12 +361,15 @@ """ def __init__( - self, text: str, handler: Optional[Callable[[], None]] = None, width: int = 12 + self, text: str, handler: Optional[Callable[[], None]] = None, width: int = 12, + left_symbol: str = "<", right_symbol: str = ">", ) -> None: self.text = text self.handler = handler self.width = width + self.left_symbol = left_symbol + self.right_symbol = right_symbol self.control = FormattedTextControl( self._get_text_fragments, key_bindings=self._get_key_bindings(), @@ -400,10 +403,10 @@ self.handler() return [ - ("class:button.arrow", "<", handler), + ("class:button.arrow", self.left_symbol, handler), ("[SetCursorPosition]", ""), ("class:button.text", text, handler), - ("class:button.arrow", ">", handler), + ("class:button.arrow", self.right_symbol, handler), ] def _get_key_bindings(self) -> KeyBindings:

requirements.description:
`create_pipe_input()` currently returns a plain `Input` object (either
      `PosixPipeInput` or `Win32PipeInput`). Test code uses it as a context manager:

          with create_pipe_input() as pipe_input:
              ...

      Because neither `create_pipe_input()` nor the returned object implements the
      context-manager protocol (`__enter__` / `__exit__`), every call inside a
      `with` statement raises `AttributeError: __enter__`. This single deficiency
      causes the entire test_cli.py suite (~30 tests) and all tests in
      test_key_binding.py (~5 tests) to fail.
requirements.contract:
`create_pipe_input()` must return an object that supports the context-manager
      protocol. When used as a context manager:
      - `__enter__` must yield (or return) the underlying pipe-input instance.
      - `__exit__` must call `close()` on the pipe input and suppress no exceptions.

      One acceptable approach: make `create_pipe_input()` a context-manager factory
      (e.g., using `contextlib.contextmanager`) that creates the appropriate
      `PosixPipeInput` or `Win32PipeInput`, yields it, and closes it in a `finally`
      block.

      An alternative approach: add `__enter__` / `__exit__` methods directly to
      `PosixPipeInput` (and `Win32PipeInput` where relevant), where `__enter__`
      returns `self` and `__exit__` calls `self.close()`.

      The object yielded/returned inside the `with` block must still be a fully
      functional `Input` instance (i.e., `send_text`, `send_bytes`, and all other
      existing methods must keep working).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
All tests in tests/test_key_binding.py (test_feed_simple, test_feed_several,
      test_common_prefix, test_remove_bindings, test_previous_key_sequence,
      test_control_square_closed_any) and all tests in tests/test_cli.py that rely
      on `set_dummy_app()` / `create_pipe_input()` as a context manager must pass
      without `AttributeError: __enter__`.

requirements.location:
prompt_toolkit/styles/base.py — `Attrs` NamedTuple definition and
      `DEFAULT_ATTRS` constant; also any callers that construct `Attrs` explicitly
      (e.g., prompt_toolkit/styles/style.py, prompt_toolkit/styles/defaults.py,
      prompt_toolkit/output/*, etc.)
requirements.description:
The `Attrs` NamedTuple currently has 8 fields:
        color, bgcolor, bold, underline, italic, blink, reverse, hidden

      Tests create `Attrs` instances with a `strike` keyword argument positioned
      between `underline` and `italic`:

          Attrs(color=..., bgcolor=..., bold=..., underline=..., strike=...,
                italic=..., blink=..., reverse=..., hidden=...)

      Because `strike` is absent from the definition, any direct construction of
      `Attrs` with `strike=` raises `TypeError: Attrs.__new__() got an unexpected
      keyword argument 'strike'`. This causes failures in test_style.py
      (test_style_from_dict, test_class_combinations_1, test_class_combinations_2,
      test_substyles, test_swap_light_and_dark_style_transformation) and
      test_style_transformation.py (test_adjust_brightness_style_transformation).
requirements.contract:
Add a `strike` field (type `Optional[bool]`) to the `Attrs` NamedTuple,
      inserted between `underline` and `italic` to match the order used in the
      tests:

          color, bgcolor, bold, underline, strike, italic, blink, reverse, hidden

      `DEFAULT_ATTRS` must be updated to include `strike=False`.

      Style parsing logic (wherever style strings like `"bold underline strike
      italic"` are processed) must recognise the token `"strike"` and set
      `strike=True` in the resulting `Attrs`.

      All internal code that constructs `Attrs` instances positionally or by keyword
      must be updated to include the new `strike` field, so that no existing
      functionality breaks.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_style.py::test_style_from_dict,
      tests/test_style.py::test_class_combinations_1,
      tests/test_style.py::test_class_combinations_2,
      tests/test_style.py::test_substyles,
      tests/test_style.py::test_swap_light_and_dark_style_transformation,
      tests/test_style_transformation.py::test_adjust_brightness_style_transformation
      must all pass without TypeError or ValueError related to `strike`.

requirements.description:
The current `History.load(item_loaded_callback)` signature requires a
      callback and is a regular synchronous method. Tests expect `load()` to be
      callable with no arguments and to behave as an **async generator** that
      yields history strings one by one:

          async for item in history.load():
              result.append(item)

      Calling the current implementation without the callback raises:
        `TypeError: History.load() missing 1 required positional argument:
        'item_loaded_callback'`

      This affects tests/test_history.py: test_in_memory_history, test_file_history,
      test_threaded_file_history, test_threaded_in_memory_history.
requirements.contract:
`History.load()` must be redefined as an **async generator method** that takes
      no arguments and yields history strings (newest first, consistent with
      existing `load_history_strings()` ordering). Example call pattern:

          async for item in history.load():
              # item is a str
              ...

      Specifically:
      - The base class `History.load()` must be an async generator (decorated with
        `async def` and use `yield` to emit items).
      - Items must be yielded newest-first (same order returned by
        `load_history_strings()`).
      - On repeated calls, already-loaded items must still be yielded (the in-memory
        cache `_loaded_strings` should be consulted on subsequent calls).
      - `ThreadedHistory.load()` must also conform to the same async generator
        contract. Since `ThreadedHistory` runs loading in a thread, it must bridge
        the synchronous thread-based loading into async iteration (e.g., using a
        queue or similar mechanism).
      - Internal callers of the old callback-based `load()` (e.g., inside
        `Buffer`) must be updated to use the new async generator interface, or a
        compatible compatibility shim must be provided so that the rest of the
        application continues to work correctly.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_history.py::test_in_memory_history,
      tests/test_history.py::test_file_history,
      tests/test_history.py::test_threaded_file_history,
      tests/test_history.py::test_threaded_in_memory_history
      must all pass. Ordering assertions ("newest first") must be satisfied.

requirements.description:
`merge_completers(completers)` accepts no `deduplicate` keyword argument.
      The test passes `deduplicate=True` or `deduplicate=False` to control whether
      duplicate completions (same `text`) are filtered out before being returned.
      Calling the function with this argument raises:
        `TypeError: merge_completers() got an unexpected keyword argument 'deduplicate'`
requirements.contract:
`merge_completers` signature must be extended to accept an optional boolean
      keyword argument `deduplicate` (default `False`):

          def merge_completers(
              completers: Sequence[Completer],
              deduplicate: bool = False,
          ) -> Completer: ...

      Behaviour:
      - When `deduplicate=False` (default): existing behaviour is preserved —
        completions from all sub-completers are yielded in order, including
        duplicates.
      - When `deduplicate=True`: completions with the same `text` value that have
        already been yielded must be suppressed. The first occurrence (from the
        first matching sub-completer) is kept.

      The deduplication must apply to both the synchronous `get_completions` path
      and the async `get_completions_async` path.

      Test expectations (with two WordCompleters sharing "abc" and "def",
      and the second also containing two "xyz" entries):
      - `deduplicate=False`: 8 completions total.
      - `deduplicate=True`: 5 completions total (hello, world, abc, def, xyz).

requirements.description:
`Button.__init__` only accepts `text`, `handler`, and `width`. The test
      instantiates a button with custom bracket symbols:

          Button("Exit", left_symbol="[", right_symbol="]")

      This raises:
        `TypeError: Button.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'left_symbol'`

      The expected rendered text is `"[   Exit   ]"` instead of the default
      `"<   Exit   >"`.
requirements.contract:
`Button.__init__` must accept two additional optional string keyword
      arguments:
      - `left_symbol: str` — the character(s) rendered to the left of the button
        label. Default: `"<"` (i.e., the `<` character, preserving current
        appearance).
      - `right_symbol: str` — the character(s) rendered to the right of the button
        label. Default: `">"`.

      The internal text-fragment generation method (`_get_text_fragments` or
      equivalent) must use these symbol values instead of hard-coded `"<"` / `">"`.

      When `left_symbol="["` and `right_symbol="]"` are passed, the rendered text
      for `Button("Exit")` (default width 12) must be `"[   Exit   ]"`.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_widgets.py::test_custom_button must pass (rendered text equals
      `"[   Exit   ]"`).
      tests/test_widgets.py::test_defaulf_button must continue to pass (rendered
      text equals `"<   Exit   >"`).

requirements.description:
当前 Vt100_Output.__init__ 在 write_binary=True 时执行硬性断言
      `assert hasattr(stdout, "encoding")`（第 422-423 行）。测试中使用的
      _Capture 伪 stdout 对象没有 encoding 属性，导致该断言直接抛出
      AssertionError，使所有依赖 print_formatted_text 的测试失败。
      同时，第 478 行的 encoding 属性和第 645 行的编码使用也需要能在
      stdout 缺少 encoding 时正常工作。
requirements.contract:
- Vt100_Output.__init__ 不得因 stdout 缺少 encoding 属性而抛出
        AssertionError；当 stdout 没有 encoding 属性时，应以 "utf-8"
        作为默认编码，而不是强制要求该属性存在。
      - Vt100_Output.encoding 属性（第 478 行附近）应返回
        getattr(self.stdout, "encoding", "utf-8") 或等价的安全访问方式。
      - 第 645 行编码写入时，应同样使用带默认值的安全访问，避免
        AttributeError。
      - 行为契约：write_binary=True 时，如果 stdout 提供了 encoding 属性则
        尊重其值；如果没有，则静默使用 "utf-8"，不报错。

requirements.description:
问题 A（test_ansi_interpolation）：ANSI 类缺少 __mod__ 方法，导致
      `ANSI("...") % "some_string"` 抛出
      "TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for %: 'ANSI' and 'str'"。
      HTML 类已有类似的 __mod__ 实现（html.py 第 115-123 行），ANSI 类同样
      需要该运算符支持。

      问题 B（test_ansi_true_color）：真彩色（24-bit）解析时，颜色值格式化
      为 "%02x%02x%02x"（例如 "90ee90"），缺少 "#" 前缀，而测试期望的格式
      为 "#90ee90"。根本原因在 _select_graphic_rendition 方法中
      color_str 的构造逻辑（约第 173-187 行）。
requirements.contract:
问题 A — __mod__ 方法契约：
      - ANSI 类需提供 __mod__(self, value) 方法。
      - 当 value 不是 tuple 时，将其包装为单元素 tuple。
      - 对 value 中的每个字符串元素应用 ansi_escape()（与 format() 中
        对参数的处理一致，用于转义嵌套 ANSI 转义码）。
      - 返回新的 ANSI 对象，其 value 为 self.value % escaped_value。
      - 签名：def __mod__(self, value: Union[object, Tuple[object, ...]]) -> "ANSI"

      问题 B — 真彩色前缀契约：
      - _select_graphic_rendition 方法在解析 38;2;R;G;B 或 48;2;R;G;B 格式
        时，生成的颜色字符串必须以 "#" 开头，格式为 "#rrggbb"（6 位十六进制）。
      - self._color 和 self._bgcolor 存储的值格式应为 "#rrggbb"。

requirements.description:
当输入文本存在前导空格时（例如 " show i"），get_completions 方法先通过
      lstrip() 得到去除前导空格的 text（"show i"），正确提取了
      first_term（"show"）。但随后计算剩余文本时使用的是
      `document.text[len(first_term):]`，而 document.text 仍包含原始前导空格
      （" show i"），导致偏移量错位：`" show i"[4:]` 得到 `"w i"` 而非正确
      的 `" i"`，进而使子 completer 无法正确补全，返回空集合。
requirements.contract:
- 计算剩余文本时，必须以去除前导空格后的 text（即 lstripped 版本）
        来确定 first_term 在文档中的实际起始偏移，而不是直接使用
        document.text 的长度减去 first_term 的长度。
      - 正确的剩余文本计算方式：找到 first_term 在 document.text 中实际
        出现的位置（考虑前导空格），截取 first_term 之后的内容。
      - 对于输入 " show i"，子 completer 接收到的 remaining_text 应能使其
        补全出以 "i" 开头的条目（"ip" 和 "interfaces"）。
      - 此修复不应影响无前导空格时的正常行为。
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_completion.py::test_nested_completer
      （特别是 Document(" show i") 的断言：{c.text for c in completions} == {"ip", "interfaces"}）

requirements.description:
当同一 Filter 对象与其自身进行 & 或 | 运算时（即 cond & cond 或
      cond | cond），当前实现直接创建 _AndList([cond, cond]) 或
      _OrList([cond, cond])，结果类型为 _AndList/_OrList 而非 Condition，
      且包含重复项。测试期望：
      - `cond1 & cond1` 的结果是 Condition 实例（即返回 cond1 本身）
      - `cond1 | cond1` 的结果是 Condition 实例（即返回 cond1 本身）
      - `cond1 & cond1 & cond1` 也是 Condition 实例
      - `cond1 & cond1 & cond2` 是 _AndList，且 .filters 长度为 2（无重复）
requirements.contract:
- _AndCache.__missing__(self, key=(a, b))：在已有的 Always/Never 短路
        判断之后，增加：若 a is b，则直接返回 a（不创建 _AndList）。
      - _OrCache.__missing__(self, key=(a, b))：同理，若 a is b，则直接返回 a。
      - 这两处短路判断应在创建 _AndList/_OrList 之前进行。
      - 返回值若为原始 Condition（即 a 本身），则 isinstance(result, Condition)
        为 True，满足测试预期。
      - 此修改不影响 a is not b 时的现有行为。

requirements.description:
HTML.format() 方法当前对所有参数调用 html_escape()，而 html_escape()
      会将参数先转换为 str 再进行 HTML 转义。当原始参数为非字符串类型（如
      int）且格式字符串中使用了类型相关的格式规范（如 {:d}）时，会因参数
      已被转换为 str 而报错：
      "ValueError: Unknown format code 'd' for object of type 'str'"。
requirements.contract:
- HTML.format(*args, **kwargs) 应能正确处理非字符串参数（如 int、float）
        搭配类型相关格式规范（如 {:d}、{:f}、{:03d} 等）的情形。
      - 正确的行为：先对原始参数使用其原生类型执行格式化（以支持格式规范），
        然后对格式化结果进行 HTML 转义；或等价地，以不丢失类型信息的方式
        完成格式化并确保输出结果被安全转义。
      - 返回值仍为 HTML 对象，其 value 为格式化并转义后的字符串。
      - 对字符串类型参数的转义行为应保持不变（即字符串值中的
        &、<、>、" 等仍被正确转义）。

requirements.description:
When a tuple of keys is matched (e.g., the ANSI sequence "\x1b[1;3D" maps to
      (Keys.Escape, Keys.Left)), _call_handler currently passes the same insert_text
      string to every key in the tuple via recursive calls. As a result, all generated
      KeyPress objects carry the full raw input string as their data field.

      The test test_special_double_keys asserts that for a tuple match the FIRST key
      should receive the original insert_text ("\x1b[1;3D") while SUBSEQUENT keys
      should receive an empty string ("") as their data.

      Current signature:
        def _call_handler(self, key: Union[str, Keys, Tuple[Keys, ...]], insert_text: str) -> None

      Current behaviour when key is a tuple:
        for k in key:
            self._call_handler(k, insert_text)   # same insert_text for all items
requirements.contract:
The updated _call_handler must satisfy the following contract when key is a
      tuple of Keys values:

      - The first element of the tuple is dispatched with the original insert_text.
      - Every subsequent element (index >= 1) is dispatched with an empty string "".
      - The recursive call for each individual key must ultimately create a
        KeyPress(key, data) and invoke self.feed_key_callback with it.
      - The behaviour for non-tuple keys (single str or Keys value) remains unchanged.
      - No changes to the method signature are required.

      Post-condition (expressed as observable KeyPress objects emitted):
        Input: stream.feed("\x1b[1;3D")
        Emitted[0]: KeyPress(key=Keys.Escape, data="\x1b[1;3D")
        Emitted[1]: KeyPress(key=Keys.Left,   data="")
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_inputstream.py::test_special_double_keys

      Specifically, all four assertions in that test must pass:
        assert len(processor.keys) == 2
        assert processor.keys[0].key == Keys.Escape
        assert processor.keys[1].key == Keys.Left
        assert processor.keys[0].data == "\x1b[1;3D"
        assert processor.keys[1].data == ""

      Regression guard: the following tests must continue to pass after the change:
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_control_keys
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_arrows
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_escape
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_flush_1
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_flush_2
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_meta_arrows
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_control_square_close
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_invalid
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_cpr_response
        tests/test_inputstream.py::test_cpr_response_2
